Planning for a Resilient Future

Understanding the complexity between built and natural systems in the face of climate change and extreme weather events.

Explore Our Research

About iAdapt

With ever-increasing worldwide risks from climate change, sea-level rise, and extreme weather events will greatly threaten the built and natural environment across the globe. iAdapt serves as an international knowledge-to-action exchange hub.

Our Core Objectives

1. Advance AI-enabled Urban Planning

Addressing Urban Planning AI related social and ethical issues, and initiating the development of Urban Planning AI.

2. Optimize Climate Adaptation Planning

Evaluating adaptation strategies in response to climate change and examining the impacts on coastal communities.

3. Characterize Urban Transportation

Assessing the impacts of autonomous public transportation systems, enhancing microtransit services, and examining interactions between shared mobility and spatial structure.

4. Enable Knowledge-to-Action Exchange

Building a bridge between the University of Florida and practice communities to cultivate professional adaptation planners.

Featured Research

Highlighting our latest projects and studies in adaptation planning.

Impact of Climate Change

A parameterized climate change projection model for hurricane flooding, wave action, economic damages, and population dynamics.

Microtransit and Micromobility

Inventory in the State of Florida. Assessing the needs, implementation processes, and examining interactions with spatial structures.

AV-based Microtransit Service

Examining data needs and implementation process of AV-based microtransit service: a case study in Lake Nona.

iAdapt Research Center

University of Florida
